session.title: AI-Powered Learning Ecosystem Architecture & xAPI Learning Evidence Design
session.topic_summary: A comprehensive architectural design session for an open-source, AI-integrated learning ecosystem spanning OpenWebUI, Moodle, a Learning Record Store, Keycloak SSO, PeerTube, and a custom xAPI bridge service. The learner drove a product vision for replacing the traditional LMS experience with AI-delivered instruction across three modalities, and identified a meaningful gap in standard xAPI — proposing enriched learning evidence as an extension layer. The session culminated in a working Docker Compose stack, a custom xAPI bridge microservice, and a structured GitHub repository scaffold.

axes.label: Systems Thinking
axes.description: Ability to design and reason about interconnected technical systems at scale

axes.label: Strategic Vision
axes.description: Clarity of product vision and ability to articulate market differentiation

axes.label: Technical Depth
axes.description: Understanding of underlying technologies, protocols, and integration patterns

axes.label: Learning Science
axes.description: Application of learning theory and pedagogy to product design decisions

axes.label: Innovation Index
axes.description: Identifying novel solutions and gaps in existing standards (xAPI extension proposal)

axes.label: Exec Communication
axes.description: Ability to frame technical decisions in business and stakeholder terms

blooms_progression.label: Remember
blooms_progression.title: Terminology Anchoring
blooms_progression.description: Confirmed understanding of POC definition when unclear; demonstrated familiarity with SSO, xAPI, MCP, LRS, Docker, and Bloom's Taxonomy without needing explanation.

blooms_progression.label: Understand
blooms_progression.title: Concept Translation
blooms_progression.description: Articulated the distinction between activity-based xAPI records ('Sean watched Video X') and learning-evidence records ('Sean demonstrated proficiency at level N') — reframing a standards limitation as a product opportunity.

blooms_progression.label: Apply
blooms_progression.title: Schema in Context
blooms_progression.description: Applied the existing Learning Intelligence JSON schema as evidence for the xAPI extension proposal, and shared real multi-platform session files (Claude, Copilot, Perplexity, Gemini) as test data — applying the schema across contexts.

blooms_progression.label: Analyze
blooms_progression.title: Architecture Decomposition
blooms_progression.description: Broke the product vision into three distinct learning modalities (pure AI, hybrid AI+instructor, AI+cohort) and analyzed how each maps to different infrastructure requirements. Also independently identified that the Moodle webservice_mcp plugin eliminated the need for custom middleware — a significant architecture simplification.

blooms_progression.label: Evaluate
blooms_progression.title: Critical Technology Judgment
blooms_progression.description: Evaluated Ollama on CPU as acceptable for POC but inadequate for production; assessed data sovereignty requirements for military/defense clients; correctly prioritized the Moodle→OpenWebUI MCP connection as the most strategically important integration to validate.

blooms_progression.label: Create
blooms_progression.title: Ecosystem Synthesis
blooms_progression.description: Directed the design of a complete 8-service Docker Compose ecosystem, custom xAPI bridge microservice, GitHub repository scaffold, and the conceptual architecture for AI-as-instructor across three modalities — synthesizing learning theory, open-source tooling, enterprise requirements, and proprietary schema work into a coherent product.

timeline.title: Vision Framing
timeline.description: Learner articulated the full product vision — SSO-unified learning ecosystem across OpenWebUI, Moodle, LRS, and MCP — including the explicit requirement for open-source tooling and enterprise scalability from the first prompt.

timeline.title: xAPI Gap Identification
timeline.description: Learner identified the critical limitation of standard xAPI (activity records vs. learning evidence) and proposed extending the spec with the Learning Intelligence JSON schema — an original conceptual contribution to the architecture.

timeline.title: Schema Validation
timeline.description: Shared 4 real session JSON files from different AI platforms (Claude, Copilot, Perplexity, Gemini) demonstrating that the schema is already cross-platform validated and production-ready as a data standard.

timeline.title: Modality Framework Creation
timeline.description: Articulated a three-modality framework for AI-delivered education (pure AI instruction, hybrid AI+human, AI+cohort) and connected it to infrastructure requirements — effectively designing the product's pedagogical architecture.

timeline.title: MCP Plugin Discovery
timeline.description: Identified the existing webservice_mcp Moodle plugin, which eliminated the need for custom middleware and significantly simplified the architecture — demonstrating proactive technical research during the session.

timeline.title: Ecosystem Commissioning
timeline.description: Directed the creation of a complete Docker Compose stack, xAPI bridge microservice, GitHub repository scaffold, and GitHub-ready documentation — moving from concept to deployable POC infrastructure within a single session.

employer_value.title: Enterprise Learning Architecture
employer_value.description: Designed a complete, scalable, open-source learning ecosystem architecture from first principles — demonstrating the systems thinking and technical depth required to lead edtech infrastructure projects at enterprise scale.

employer_value.title: Standards Innovation
employer_value.description: Identified a meaningful gap in the xAPI specification and proposed an evidence-based extension model — demonstrating the ability to engage with and advance industry standards, not merely implement them.

employer_value.title: Product Vision Clarity
employer_value.description: Articulated a differentiated product vision (AI as live instructor, LMS as backend record system) with clear market positioning against traditional edtech — including identifying defensible competitive moats for defense/enterprise clients.

employer_value.title: Full-Stack Technical Literacy
employer_value.description: Demonstrated working knowledge across authentication (Keycloak/OIDC), containerization (Docker Compose), LMS integration (Moodle MCP), LLM deployment (Ollama, GPU scaling), and data standards (xAPI, JSON schema) — rare breadth for a learning professional.

employer_value.title: Proprietary Competency Measurement
employer_value.description: Brings a functional, cross-platform competency measurement schema and dashboard system — an original intellectual asset that replaces LMS completion data with verifiable learning evidence.

portfolio.title: AI Learning Ecosystem Design & xAPI Evidence Architecture
portfolio.subtitle: SELF-DIRECTED · APPLIED · DEPLOYABLE
portfolio.secondary_title: EdTech Product Strategy & Distance Education Redesign

meta.notes: Session transcript is complete and detailed. Duration estimated at 55 minutes based on conversation length and technical depth. Scores reflect a learner operating at expert-adjacent level in learning systems design with genuine original contributions (xAPI extension proposal, three-modality pedagogical framework). The meta-cognition score (82) is slightly tempered because self-reflection was primarily implicit rather than explicitly verbalized — the learner was in execution mode throughout. ROI multiplier (24x) reflects compression of what would be weeks of independent research, architecture consulting, and tool evaluation into a single session.
